articles.title: Judges Visit Rochdale for RHS Competition
articles.excerpt: Judges from the RHS and NWIB visited Rochdale to assess the area as part of a competition, touring key locations including Castleton train station and Denehurst Park.
articles.content_html:
Judges from the Royal Horticultural Society (RHS) and the North West in Bloom (NWIB) visited Rochdale on 21 July 2026 as part of a regional competition.

The judges were taken on a tour that included notable locations such as Castleton train station, Denehurst Park, and Rochdale town centre.

The visit aimed to evaluate the area's floral displays and community efforts in horticulture, contributing to the overall assessment for the competition.

Rochdale in Bloom expressed gratitude for the judges' visit and the opportunity to showcase the town's efforts in enhancing its green spaces.

articles.title: Scaffolding Project Completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve
articles.excerpt: A scaffolding project has been completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve to facilitate bridge restoration works. The project involved erecting scaffolding for waterproof encapsulation.
articles.content_html:
A scaffolding project has been completed at Healey Dell Nature Reserve, Rochdale. The scaffolding was erected to provide a waterproof encapsulation to enable bridge restoration works.

The project highlights the ongoing efforts to maintain and improve local natural sites, ensuring they remain accessible and safe for visitors.

Local contractors undertook the work, which is expected to enhance the structural integrity of the bridge and prolong its lifespan.

The completion of this project may mean improved access for walkers and nature enthusiasts visiting the reserve.

articles.title: Rochdale Weather Forecast: Sunny Intervals and Mild Temperatures
articles.excerpt: The Met Office has issued a weather forecast for Rochdale, predicting sunny intervals and mild temperatures over the coming days.
articles.content_html:
The Met Office has released the latest weather forecast for Rochdale, indicating a week of mild temperatures and sunny intervals. Today, temperatures are expected to reach a maximum of 21 degrees Celsius, with a minimum of 11 degrees overnight.

On Sunday, the forecast predicts a high of 21 degrees Celsius with sunny intervals and a low of 13 degrees. Rain is unlikely, with less than a 5% chance of precipitation throughout the day.

Monday and Tuesday are expected to see similar conditions, with highs of 23 degrees Celsius and a slight chance of rain in the afternoon. Wind gusts could reach up to 18 mph on Sunday and 15 mph on Monday.

The forecast for the rest of the week shows temperatures gradually decreasing, with highs of 22 degrees on Wednesday and 21 degrees on Thursday, before dropping to 19 degrees on Friday. Residents may wish to take advantage of the sunny weather while it lasts.

articles.title: Rochdale Council Staff Acknowledged for Their Support
articles.excerpt: Three members of the Environmental Management Department at Rochdale Borough Council received public thanks for their assistance during a recent incident.
articles.content_html:
A post on social media has expressed gratitude towards three members of the Environmental Management Department at Rochdale Borough Council. The staff members, named Julian, Mark, and Brett, were praised for their helpfulness during a recent incident.

The post highlighted how the three staff members 'literally came to our rescue' and noted their kindness and support. The message referred to them as 'absolute stars', reflecting the positive impact of their assistance.

This acknowledgment comes as part of ongoing community engagement, where residents are encouraged to recognise the efforts of local council staff in providing essential services.

Rochdale Borough Council continues to promote a culture of appreciation for its employees, aiming to foster strong relationships between the council and the community.

articles.title: Rochdale In Bloom Hosts North West in Bloom Judges
articles.excerpt: Rochdale In Bloom welcomed judges from North West in Bloom to the town centre this week, showcasing various local projects including Broadfield Slopes and the living roof at Number One Riverside.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale In Bloom welcomed judges from North West in Bloom to the town centre this week. The event showcased several local projects, including Broadfield Slopes, the living roof at Number One Riverside, Baillie Street Quarter, and the town hall square.

The judges assessed the efforts made by the community to enhance the local environment and promote biodiversity. The initiative aims to encourage residents and businesses to take pride in their surroundings.

This year's competition highlights the importance of green spaces and community involvement in maintaining a vibrant town centre. The judges' visit is part of the annual assessment of towns participating in the North West in Bloom competition.

Results from the competition will be announced later this year, with hopes that Rochdale will receive recognition for its efforts in improving the local area.

articles.title: Healey councillor welcomes first beehive to Number One Riverside roof
articles.excerpt: Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton has welcomed the installation of the first beehive on the roof of Number One Riverside in Rochdale town centre.
articles.summary: A new rooftop beehive supplied and maintained by Boro Bees will make use of the sedum roof and nearby riverbank wildflowers, with its first honey being left for the colony over winter.
articles.content_html:
Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton has welcomed the installation of the first beehive on the roof of Number One Riverside in Rochdale town centre.

The hive was installed with support from Boro Bees, which supplied the colony and will continue to maintain it.

Councillor Ayrton described the installation as exciting news and said the bees would benefit from the building’s sedum roof and the wildflowers growing along the nearby riverbank.

Supporting bees in the town centre

The rooftop location will give the bees access to pollen and nectar from plants around Number One Riverside, the River Roch and areas further afield.

Sedum roofs are planted with low-growing vegetation and can provide additional habitats and food sources for pollinating insects in built-up areas.

The project adds a new biodiversity feature to the council building while demonstrating how urban spaces can be adapted to support wildlife.

First honey to remain with the colony

Any honey produced during the colony’s first year will be left for the bees themselves.

This will help the colony establish itself and provide the food reserves needed to survive and remain healthy through the winter.

Those involved in the project hope the hive may produce surplus honey in future years once the colony is well established.

“The bees will benefit from the sedum roof on the top of Number One Riverside and from the wildflowers that grow along the riverbank.”

Hive to be maintained by local specialists

Boro Bees will be responsible for maintaining the hive and monitoring the health of the colony.

Photographs from the installation show a beekeeper placing frames containing the bees inside the wooden rooftop hive.

This report is based on a public update and photographs published by Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton following the installation of the beehive at Number One Riverside.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports a public update issued by an elected councillor. References to the environmental benefits and future honey production of the hive reflect information shared about the project.

articles.title: Weather Forecast for Castleton: July 16, 2026
articles.excerpt: The weather forecast for Castleton, Rochdale, on July 16, 2026, predicts a mix of sun and clouds with occasional rain showers expected. Temperatures are likely to reach a high of 20 degrees Celsius, while overnight lows may drop to around 12 degrees Celsius.
articles.content_html:
The weather forecast for Castleton, Rochdale, on July 16, 2026, predicts a mix of sun and clouds with occasional rain showers expected.

Temperatures are likely to reach a high of 20 degrees Celsius, while overnight lows may drop to around 12 degrees Celsius.

People should carry umbrellas as rain is expected intermittently throughout the day, particularly in the afternoon.

For those planning travel or outdoor activities, it is advisable to check for updates on weather conditions as they may change.

articles.title: UK Faces Unprecedented Climate Change with Extreme Temperatures
articles.excerpt: The UK is experiencing unprecedented climate change, with extreme temperatures expected to persist in the coming years. Experts warn that the impacts of climate change are increasingly evident across the country.
articles.content_html:
The UK is experiencing unprecedented climate change, with extreme temperatures expected to persist in the coming years. Experts warn that the impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly evident across the country.

Recent reports indicate that the frequency and intensity of heatwaves are likely to increase, posing challenges for public health and infrastructure.

In Rochdale, residents may notice changes in weather patterns, which could affect local services and community activities.

The ongoing climate crisis highlights the need for collective action to address environmental issues and promote sustainability within communities.

articles.title: Twelve Green Spaces in Heywood Celebrate Green Flag Award Success
articles.excerpt: Twelve parks and green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines have received Green Flag Awards for another year.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 14 July 2026 at 21:47. 3 source updates have been combined into this article.

Twelve parks and green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines have received Green Flag Awards for another year.

Latest update

Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines are celebrating another year of success in the Green Flag Award scheme. This accolade highlights the quality and management of these green spaces, which are vital to the local community.

The Green Flag Award is a national standard for parks and green spaces, recognising well-managed sites that meet specific criteria. The award is a testament to the hard work of local teams and volunteers who dedicate their time to maintaining these areas throughout the year.

The success of these green spaces contributes positively to the local environment and community well-being, providing residents with accessible areas for recreation and relaxation.

This achievement reflects the ongoing commitment to enhancing the quality of life in the region, and it is hoped that the recognition will encourage further community involvement in maintaining and improving local green spaces.

Earlier developments

Twelve parks and green spaces across Rochdale borough are celebrating another year of Green Flag Award success.

The recognised sites are located across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines.

Rochdale Borough Council described the awards as a significant achievement for the employees and volunteers who work throughout the year to keep the borough’s green spaces welcoming and accessible.

The Green Flag Award is an international quality mark for parks and green spaces.

Sites are assessed against standards covering areas such as cleanliness, environmental management, biodiversity, safety, community involvement and the quality of their facilities.

The awards recognise spaces that are well managed and provide benefits for residents, visitors and local wildlife.

The council praised the teams and community volunteers who contribute to the upkeep of local parks throughout the seasons.

Their work includes maintaining paths, gardens and planted areas, caring for habitats and helping to ensure the spaces remain pleasant places for recreation and relaxation.

Update timeline

  • 14 July 2026, 21:47: Twelve Green Spaces in Heywood Celebrate Green Flag Award Success
  • 14 July 2026, 20:23: Twelve Rochdale borough green spaces retain Green Flag Awards
